prompt:
Add crash-capture / hang-recovery hardening to the NixOS host `classic-laddie` in this flake repo (cosmo). This is 'Track A' of a diagnosis effort — its goal is NOT to fix the root cause of the hangs, but to make the next hang self-recover AND leave a diagnostic trace, because right now the machine freezes completely silently and cannot record what killed it.

BACKGROUND (put a condensed version of this in the PR description):
- classic-laddie is a Ryzen 9 5950X (Zen 3, AM4, DDR4, non-ECC) + RTX 4090 desktop/media-server running NixOS.
- It has a chronic history of hard hangs: 27 unclean 'crash' reboots since March 2026, spread across 12 different kernel versions (6.12.x, 6.18.x, 6.19.x-zen). Crashes span every kernel, so this is NOT a kernel regression — it points at hardware/firmware or a constant (NVIDIA driver, RAM, PSU, C-state/idle power).
- Most recent event: on Wed 2026-07-08 07:44:48 the journal just STOPS mid-stream (routine activity, then silence) — no panic, no OOM, no shutdown targets. A classic silent hard lockup. The box then sat dead (no SSH, no monitor) for ~3 days until it was physically hard-reset.
- The reason we cannot name the cause: the machine is configured to freeze silently — nmi_watchdog is on but hardlockup_panic=0, softlockup_panic=0, no panic-on-oops, no crash capture (no ramoops/pstore/netconsole), and the periodic health check only samples hourly.

WHAT TO BUILD:
Create a new reusable module `modules/common/crash-capture.nix` that mirrors the conventions of the existing `modules/common/health.nix` EXACTLY: an `options.modules.crashCapture.enable = lib.mkEnableOption "...";` (default OFF so it does not affect the build workers / microvms), a `config = lib.mkIf cfg.enable { ... }` block, 2-space indentation, and heavy WHY-comments explaining each setting (match the commenting density of health.nix and system.nix).

The module must do three things:

1) AUTO-RECOVER + turn detectable lockups into panics (low risk, high value):
   - `boot.kernelParams = [ "panic=20" ];`  (reboot 20s after any panic instead of sitting dead forever; this list merges with the existing `boot.kernelParams = [ "usbcore.autosuspend=-1" ]` in hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix — no conflict).
   - `boot.kernel.sysctl` with: `"kernel.panic_on_oops" = 1;`, `"kernel.softlockup_panic" = 1;`, `"kernel.hardlockup_panic" = 1;`. (The NMI hard-lockup detector is already on; hardlockup_panic makes it panic-and-reboot instead of only warning.)
   - CRITICAL — DO NOT set `kernel.hung_task_panic`. Add a comment explaining why: modules/common/system.nix documents a BENIGN ~122s hung_task_timeout that fires during NVMe swap-in (the 2026-04-29 shmem_swapin_folio D-state stall, since mitigated with zram). hung_task fires on I/O D-state waits, which are expected here — panicking on it would reboot the box during heavy builds/swap. The hard/soft-lockup detectors target true CPU lockups and will not false-fire on I/O waits, so they are the safe choice.

2) HIGH-FREQUENCY TELEMETRY to the persistent journal (the highest-value, zero-risk diagnostic — this is what we lacked on Jul 8):
   - A systemd service + timer (or a single long-running service with a sleep loop) that every 30-60s logs ONE compact line to the journal under a distinct SyslogIdentifier (e.g. `crash-telemetry`, so it is filterable with `journalctl -t crash-telemetry`).
   - The line should capture: all CPU temps (use `sensors`), GPU temp+utilization (`nvidia-smi --query-gpu=temperature.gpu,utilization.gpu,memory.used --format=csv,noheader`), 1-min loadavg, mem+swap usage, and the single top CPU-consuming process. Keep it cheap. journald persists to /var/log/journal, so after the NEXT freeze the journal tail will show the thermal + load trajectory in the final seconds before death — directly answering 'was it heat / a runaway load'. Reuse tools already present via health.nix (lm_sensors, coreutils, procps); nvidia-smi comes from the NVIDIA driver — guard the call so the unit still works if nvidia-smi is momentarily unavailable.

3) BEST-EFFORT PERSISTENT PANIC CAPTURE (only if you can do it safely and prove it builds):
   - Enable systemd's pstore archiving so a panic captured by any pstore backend is copied to /var/lib/systemd/pstore on next boot. If this AM4 board exposes ACPI ERST, that suffices; many consumer AM4 boards do not, so ALSO consider a small ramoops (pstore/ram) region so the warm reboot triggered by panic=20 preserves the panic dmesg in /sys/fs/pstore. Ramoops on x86 needs a reserved physical address (mem_address/mem_size) which is board-specific and risky — if you are NOT confident you can reserve a region safely, OMIT ramoops entirely and instead document 'netconsole to another always-on LAN host' as the recommended manual follow-up in the PR body. Do NOT ship a fragile pinned-address guess. The telemetry-to-journal in (2) is already a solid reboot-surviving capture path, so it is fine if (3) ends up being pstore-only or documented-only.

WIRE IT UP:
- Import `modules/common/crash-capture.nix` and set `modules.crashCapture.enable = true;` for classic-laddie, mirroring EXACTLY how `modules/common/health.nix` is imported and enabled on this host (locate that wiring first — health runs on the box, so follow the identical path, whether that is the host default.nix imports list or a common aggregator). Enable it ONLY on classic-laddie in this PR.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Match repo conventions from AGENTS.md: Nix, 2-space indent, declarative, prefer modules over ad-hoc scripts, explicit imports. No direct commits to main (a PR branch is created for you).
- Do not modify unrelated config. Keep the diff focused.

VALIDATION (do ALL of this BEFORE opening the PR; fix anything that fails):
- Build the full host derivation (this is the real integration test): `nix build .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel` — must succeed.
- Prove the settings actually land by evaluating the built config:
  * `nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.boot.kernelParams` must contain "panic=20" (and still contain "usbcore.autosuspend=-1").
  * `nix eval --json .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.boot.kernel.sysctl` must contain kernel.softlockup_panic, kernel.hardlockup_panic, kernel.panic_on_oops = 1, and MUST NOT contain kernel.hung_task_panic.
  * Confirm your telemetry service + timer units exist in the evaluated config.
- If the repo has pre-commit hooks (.pre-commit-config) or a fast `nix flake check`, run them and fix failures.
- Do NOT run `nixos-rebuild switch`/activate or otherwise touch the running system — building the toplevel derivation is sufficient and safe.

PR DESCRIPTION must include: the condensed background above; a table/list of each setting and why; an explicit note that hung_task_panic was deliberately excluded (with the reason); how to verify after deploy (`journalctl -t crash-telemetry`, `cat /proc/sys/kernel/softlockup_panic`, check /sys/fs/pstore after a future panic); and a 'Recommended follow-ups (out of scope)' section listing Track B items: netconsole to another always-on LAN host for guaranteed hard-hang capture, and the 5950X idle-freeze BIOS mitigations (Power Supply Idle Control = Typical Current Idle, update BIOS/AGESA, loosen/disable DDR4 XMP) plus an overnight memtest86+ run.
